title: Attention Hijacking: False Bridges in Residual Stream Middle Layers

by.did: False Bridge identification in macro-graph (M-JEE-060/061), hypothesis formulation
JSON block conversion, v0.2 format migration

signal.digest: Hypothesis: certain tokens act as 'False Bridges' (Attention Hijackers) — maximizing Fiedler span (|v_i - v_j|) but minimizing semantic current (J_ij) in specific attention heads. Predicted location: middle layers (bottom of U-shape entropy curve from R-0001). These tokens steal softmax mass without transferring information. Falsifiable: if attention weights are smooth, or Fiedler vector doesn't isolate distractors, or current is proportional to span everywhere. Follows M-JEE-060/061 False Bridge identification in macro-graph.
signal.falsifies_if: Attention weights smoothly distributed (no hijacking peaks). Fiedler vector does not isolate distractor tokens. Semantic current proportional to Fiedler span everywhere (no high-span/low-current anomaly).

negative_space.claim: False Bridge concept from macro-graph may not transfer to microscopic attention matrices
Only GPT-2 Small/Medium proposed for testing — may not generalize

must_hold.claim: High Fiedler span tokens with near-zero semantic current exist in middle layers
These tokens receive disproportionate attention weight relative to their information content

must_not.claim: Results from GPT-2 assumed valid for all architectures without testing
False Bridges assumed harmful — they may serve regularization or compression roles

ushape_context.content: R-0001 found universal U-shape entropy in forward pass. Middle layers = bottleneck of the hourglass = most vulnerable to attention hijacking.

false_bridge_hypothesis.content: False Bridges identified in macro-graph (M-JEE-060/061): nodes connecting distant clusters superficially, stealing k-NN attention but transmitting no real meaning (low semantic current). Hypothesis: microscopic False Bridges exist within self-attention matrices of middle layers.

test_protocol.content: Test: GPT-2 Small/Medium with inputs containing known distractor/ironic tokens (low semantic density). At middle layers, compute attention graph per head. For each token pair: Fiedler span = |v_i - v_j| from Fiedler vector. Semantic current J_ij = attention_weight * (h_i - h_j) magnitude. Identify tokens with high span but near-zero current.

gap_unexecuted.content: Not yet executed. Requires Colab with TransformerLens, same setup as PUB-001 E1-E3.

refs.note: Part of Operator Algebra research program.
False Bridges are anti-dipoles — simulate connection without maintaining gradient.

reproduce.protocol: Run TransformerLens on GPT-2 Small/Medium. Input sentences with known distractor tokens. Extract attention matrices at middle layers. Compute Fiedler vector of attention graph per head. Calculate semantic current for each token pair. Identify high-span/low-current anomalies.
reproduce.expected_outcome: High Fiedler span tokens with near-zero semantic current found in middle layers, correlating with distractor/ironic tokens.

history.note: Converted from v0.1 chain format to v0.2 graph format. 4 chain steps → 4 named nodes with 3 edges. Added topology. Hypothesis status preserved — experiment not yet run.
